title: The Far Shore Unmarked

features.name: wet-ground-trace
features.desc-on-touch: Footprints that fill with water as you watch, erasing themselves. This shore remembers nothing that arrives on it.

features.name: washed-wrack-line
features.desc-on-touch: Debris from upstream arranged in patterns too neat to be accident, too broken to be meaning.

features.name: unmarked-grass
features.desc-on-touch: Sedge and reed that marks no territory. It grows parallel to the water, neither advancing nor retreating.

features.name: shore-mist
features.desc-on-touch: Fog that holds the memory of the crossing — you could not see the far bank as you crossed. Now you cannot see back.

features.name: drift-line-markers
features.desc-on-touch: Sticks arranged in a line, purpose unknown. Recent. Not here before the crossing?

features.name: undertow-mouth
features.desc-on-touch: A small gulch where current exits the river. It hums with water-memory, channels of the crossing made physical.

inscription: the ground here accepts your weight but forgets you walked. are you truly here, or only half-remembered by the current?

file.name: Arrival_Recording_FirstLight_Unmarked.txt
file.note: Leif's field notes from the crossing, tide-damaged, portions illegible. Katita's signature on the margin in pencil.

reverie.title: REVERIE — far shore after crossing
narr: You stand in shallows, legs cold from the crossing,
and the opposite bank — where you came from — dissolves
into morning mist. Here there are no footprints yet.
She is already stepping inland, reading unmarked ground
like text no one wrote. You follow, dripping, into the
forgetting she said would happen on the other side.

exits.label: back across the water to the bend where you crossed
where the shore extends into true wilderness
where the shore hooks back and rejoins itself
back to the riverside, the other bank's perspective
